title: pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. lindleyana
intro: Pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. lindleyana, also known as Rocky Mountain Douglas-fir, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the western United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. lindleyana include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function, among others. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its needles or bark, and can be used in the form of teas, tinctures, or infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
